LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PERDIX INC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JUAN KAUIL IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PERDIX'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. MR. KAUIL HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT HE WAS ABLE TO PERFORM THE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF THE JOB WITH OR WITHOUT ACCOMMODATION OR THAT HE SUFFERED AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, MR. KAUIL IS GRANTED LEAVE TO CORRECT THESE DEFICIENCIES. SINCE THE FACTS SOUGHT TO BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED ARE NEITHER BEYOND DISPUTE NOR EASILY VERIFIABLE, PERDIX'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-09-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Continued To May-09-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PERDIX, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2017 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PERDIX INC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the sixth and seventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Juan Kauil is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Perdix's request for judicial notice is denied. Mr. Kauil has not sufficiently alleged that he was able to perform the essential functions of the job with or without accommodation or that he suffered an adverse employment action. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Kauil is granted leave to correct these deficiencies. Since the facts sought to be judicially noticed are neither beyond dispute nor easily verifiable, Perdix's request for judicial notice is denied. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
